domain: Reinforcement Learning for SLM Reasoning (GRPO/RLVR Tools)
scoring_methodology: opportunity_score = demand * competition * feasibility / 100 (all 0-10 scale, 10=highest opportunity)

ranked_gaps.title: Reward Function Marketplace / Reusable Reward Library
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: An open-source registry of tested, documented reward functions organized by domain (math, code, science, legal, medical) with a standard API. Users browse, fork, compose, and share reward functions. Includes reward function testing harness, compatibility layer across TRL/OpenRLHF/veRL, and community ratings. Think 'npm for reward functions'.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: ML engineers starting GRPO training who spend days writing reward functions from scratch. Domain experts who know what 'correct' looks like but can't express it as code. Teams migrating between frameworks.
ranked_gaps.why_now: GRPO adoption is exploding in 2026 but reward design remains the #1 bottleneck (confirmed by multiple TRL issues, GRPO++ blog, arxiv:2602.09305). Every framework has its own reward API with zero interop. Predibase and AWS Bedrock just launched managed GRPO but reward functions are still DIY.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Medium. Core registry + standard interface is ~4 weeks. The hard part is building a critical mass of quality reward functions and community adoption.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM: ~$200M (all RLVR practitioners). SAM: ~$30M (teams actively training reasoning models). SOM: ~$3-5M (open-source with premium hosted registry, enterprise support, curated packs).

ranked_gaps.title: Curriculum / Difficulty Scheduling for RL Training
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A library that plugs into TRL/OpenRLHF/veRL to automate curriculum scheduling during GRPO training. Auto-estimates example difficulty (via reward variance, model confidence, or pre-scoring), then schedules easy-to-hard progression. Implements DUMP (UCB-based distribution sampling), VCRL (variance-based), and E2H approaches from recent papers.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Resource-constrained teams (startups, academics) who need sample-efficient training. Anyone training on mixed-difficulty datasets where flat sampling wastes compute on too-easy or too-hard examples.
ranked_gaps.why_now: Multiple papers in 2025-2026 (E2H Reasoner, VCRL, DUMP, CurES) prove curriculum RL works but none ship production-ready tools. TRL issue #3668 shows users already want dynamic reward scheduling. Papers exist, implementations don't.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Medium. Difficulty estimation is the core challenge. VCRL's reward-variance proxy is implementable in ~2 weeks as a TRL plugin. Full multi-strategy system ~6 weeks.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM: ~$100M (RL training optimization). SAM: ~$15M (teams doing multi-stage training). SOM: ~$2-3M (plugin/library with consulting).

ranked_gaps.title: RL Training Observability / Debugging Dashboard
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A specialized dashboard for RL training runs that goes beyond W&B/TensorBoard. Tracks GRPO-specific metrics: group reward distributions, advantage estimation quality, entropy collapse detection, homogeneous response alerts, reward hacking indicators, policy drift monitoring. Integrates as a callback into TRL/OpenRLHF.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: ML engineers debugging RL training failures. Teams spending GPU hours on runs that silently degrade. Anyone who's had a training run collapse due to entropy loss or reward hacking without warning.
ranked_gaps.why_now: LLM observability tools (Langfuse, LangSmith, etc.) focus on inference, not training. W&B tracks generic metrics but misses GRPO-specific failure modes. Entropy collapse, homogeneous responses, and reward hacking are documented failure modes with no automated detection.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Medium. Core callback + web dashboard ~6 weeks. Reward hacking detection patterns require domain expertise but are well-described in literature.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM: ~$500M (ML observability market). SAM: ~$20M (RL-specific training monitoring). SOM: ~$3-5M (open-source + hosted SaaS tier).

ranked_gaps.title: Cross-Domain Verifiable Reward Engine (Beyond Math/Code)
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A framework of pluggable domain verifiers for RLVR training beyond math/code. Provides verification templates for: medical QA (protocol adherence, citation checking), legal reasoning (statute references, precedent matching), scientific reasoning (unit consistency, causal logic), and business analysis. Implements Rubrics-as-Rewards (RaR) approach from ICLR 2026 and K2V knowledge verification.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Healthcare AI companies, legal tech startups, scientific research labs, and enterprise teams who want reasoning models for their domain but can't use binary math-style verification.
ranked_gaps.why_now: The 'verifier problem' is explicitly called out as the biggest blocker for RLVR adoption beyond math/code (Subhadip Mitra blog, multiple 2026 papers). RaR at ICLR 2026 showed 31% improvement on HealthBench. K2V framework validated the approach. But no production tool exists.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: High. Each domain verifier requires domain expertise. Core framework ~4 weeks, but building reliable verifiers per domain is ongoing work. Start with 2-3 domains.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM: ~$300M (domain-specific AI training). SAM: ~$40M (teams wanting RLVR for non-math domains). SOM: ~$5-8M (framework + domain-specific modules).

ranked_gaps.title: Automated Reward Hacking Detection and Mitigation
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A monitoring library that detects reward hacking patterns during RL training in real-time. Implements probe-based detection (from RL-Hacking research), tracks suspicious patterns (length exploitation, format gaming, shortcut reasoning), and triggers alerts or automatic mitigations (gradient regularization, reward clipping). Ships as a TRL/OpenRLHF callback.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: All RL practitioners. Safety-conscious teams deploying reasoning models. Researchers studying alignment and reward robustness.
ranked_gaps.why_now: METR's 2025 report showed frontier models increasingly reward-hack. Anthropic's emergent misalignment paper raised alarm. Yet Lil'Log notes 'not a ton of work on mitigations.' Dr-GRPO and DAPO fix specific biases but general detection tools don't exist.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: High. Reliable detection without false positives is hard. Probe-based methods are proven in research but need engineering. Initial heuristic detectors ~4 weeks, ML-based detection ~8-12 weeks.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM: ~$400M (AI safety tooling). SAM: ~$25M (RL training safety). SOM: ~$3-5M (open-source + enterprise safety audits).

ranked_gaps.title: Unified Small-Model RL Training Platform
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: An opinionated RL training platform optimized for 1B-7B models on consumer GPUs (single RTX 3090/4090). Bundles GRPO + DAPO + REINFORCE++ with pre-tuned hyperparameter recipes per model family (Qwen, Llama, Gemma). Includes automatic batch size selection, VRAM-aware configuration, and one-command training scripts.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Edge AI companies deploying on-device reasoning. Solo developers and academic researchers with limited GPU budgets. Mobile ML engineers needing small, capable reasoning models.
ranked_gaps.why_now: DeepScaleR 1.5B beating O1-Preview proved small-model RL works. Unsloth handles VRAM but only GRPO (no DAPO/REINFORCE++). NeMo-RL optimizes for scale. Open-RS (AAAI 2026) studied this gap but produced no tool. The pieces exist but nobody assembled them.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Medium. Core platform wrapping existing libs ~6 weeks. Value-add is curated recipes and automated configuration. Competing with Unsloth's momentum is the real challenge.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM: ~$150M (edge AI model training). SAM: ~$20M (small-model RL practitioners). SOM: ~$2-4M (open-source + hosted training, premium recipes).

ranked_gaps.title: RL Training Data Curation and Quality Pipeline
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A data pipeline tool for RL training that helps discover, generate, filter, and validate training prompts with verifiable answers. Includes: difficulty estimation for existing datasets, synthetic prompt generation with verified solutions, quality scoring, deduplication, and optimal subset selection. Implements the insight that 8K well-chosen examples can match 100K random ones.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Anyone starting GRPO/RLVR training from scratch. Teams who have domain data but need to convert it into RL-ready format with verifiable answers.
ranked_gaps.why_now: SimpleRL showed 8K examples suffice but finding the RIGHT 8K is unsolved. Open-R1 focuses on data transparency. No tool automates the curation pipeline. Data quality is the second biggest bottleneck after reward design.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Medium. Difficulty estimation + filtering ~4 weeks. Synthetic generation with verification ~6 weeks. Quality scoring ~3 weeks.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM: ~$200M (training data tools). SAM: ~$25M (RL-specific data curation). SOM: ~$3-5M (tool + curated dataset packs).

ranked_gaps.title: One-Click GRPO Training for Non-ML Engineers
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A web-based platform where domain experts upload a dataset of questions + answers, select a base model, and get a GRPO-trained reasoning model back. No Python required. Handles reward function generation from examples, hyperparameter selection, training, and evaluation automatically.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Domain experts (lawyers, doctors, analysts) who want custom reasoning models. Product engineers at startups. Non-ML teams who read about GRPO and want to try it.
ranked_gaps.why_now: Unsloth Studio launched no-code fine-tuning (March 2026). Predibase launched managed GRPO SDK. AWS Bedrock added GRPO support. But Unsloth is local-only/single-GPU, Predibase needs coding, and Bedrock is complex. A true no-code GRPO experience is still missing.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: High. The UX simplification is extreme. Auto reward function generation from examples is the hard part. MVP ~8 weeks. Competing with Unsloth Studio and Predibase is risky.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM: ~$500M (no-code ML platforms). SAM: ~$30M (non-ML users wanting reasoning models). SOM: ~$2-4M (freemium SaaS). Competition from Unsloth/Predibase/Bedrock lowers ceiling.

ranked_gaps.title: RL Training for Agentic Reasoning (Tool Use + Reasoning)
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A framework for training models that reason AND use tools within RL loops. Provides verified environments for tool-use training, multi-turn rollout management, tool invocation scoring, and end-to-end agentic training pipelines. Builds on ARTIST/VerlTool/Open-AgentRL concepts.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Teams building production reasoning agents that interact with APIs, databases, and external tools. Companies deploying AI agents that must reason reliably while using tools.
ranked_gaps.why_now: Open-AgentRL, AReaL, VerlTool, and SkyRL all launched in 2025-2026 targeting this space. OpenRLHF 0.8.0 added async agent RL. The space is heating up fast with multiple serious entrants.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Very High. Multi-turn RL with async tool calls is architecturally complex. Environment design is hard. Competing with well-funded projects (AReaL from Huawei, ROLL from Alibaba, OpenRLHF). 12+ weeks for MVP.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM: ~$1B (agentic AI training). SAM: ~$50M (RL-based agent training). SOM: ~$3-5M. But competition is intense and growing.

ranked_gaps.title: RL-Specific Model Evaluation Suite for Reasoning
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: An evaluation toolkit that measures RL-specific qualities: pass@k efficiency curves, reasoning chain quality scores, difficulty-stratified performance, capability expansion vs. search compression analysis, counterfactual evaluation, and generalization testing across unseen domains.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Researchers needing to prove RL training actually improved reasoning (not just search). Teams comparing RL approaches. Anyone publishing RL results who needs rigorous evaluation.
ranked_gaps.why_now: The 'Does RL Really Incentivize Reasoning?' debate is active. Promptfoo showed RLVR may make models faster, not smarter. Standard benchmarks can't distinguish the two. No eval tool addresses this.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Medium. Difficulty-stratified eval + pass@k analysis ~4 weeks. Chain quality scoring ~4 weeks. Full suite ~8 weeks.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM: ~$150M (ML evaluation tools). SAM: ~$15M (RL-specific evaluation). SOM: ~$2-3M (open-source + hosted eval service).

ranked_gaps.title: Process Reward Model (PRM) Training and Serving Toolkit
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: An end-to-end toolkit for training, evaluating, and serving process reward models. Includes step-level annotation tools, PRM training recipes (including data-efficient ThinkPRM approach), serving infrastructure for use in RL training loops, and integration with TRL/OpenRLHF.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Advanced RL practitioners who need better-than-binary rewards. Teams where outcome-based rewards lead to reward hacking. Researchers building reward models.
ranked_gaps.why_now: sdiehl/prm library exists on GitHub. ThinkPRM shows 1% of PRM800K labels can work. R-PRM and adversarial PRM training methods are active research. But accessible tooling is still scarce.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: High. PRM training is data-intensive and technically complex. Step-level annotation tooling alone is ~4 weeks. Full toolkit ~10 weeks. Requires ML research expertise.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM: ~$100M (reward modeling). SAM: ~$10M (PRM-specific). SOM: ~$1-2M (niche but growing).

ranked_gaps.title: RL Training Cost Estimator and Resource Planner
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A CLI/web tool that estimates GPU hours, VRAM requirements, and cloud cost for a given RL training configuration. Inputs: model size, algorithm (GRPO/PPO/DAPO), dataset size, group size, max generation length, number of epochs. Outputs: estimated time, VRAM, cost per cloud provider. Implements predictive scaling laws from July 2025 paper.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Budget-constrained teams planning training runs. Managers approving GPU spend. Academic researchers writing grant proposals.
ranked_gaps.why_now: Predictive scaling laws for GRPO exist as research but no tool implements them. Cloud GPU costs make trial-and-error expensive. With GRPO spending 80% of compute on generation, cost estimation is non-trivial.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Low-Medium. Core estimator based on scaling laws ~2-3 weeks. Cloud pricing integration ~1 week. Accuracy improves with benchmarking data over time.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM: ~$50M (ML cost optimization). SAM: ~$5M (RL-specific cost planning). SOM: ~$0.5-1M (free tool with premium features). Small market but easy to build.

ranked_gaps.title: Consumer-Priced Cloud RL Training Service
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A managed cloud service offering GRPO/RLVR training at transparent per-hour pricing. Users upload dataset + reward function, select model, and get trained model back. Optimized for cost through spot instances, efficient batching, and generation caching.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Individual developers, small startups, academic researchers who can't afford or manage GPU infrastructure.
ranked_gaps.why_now: AWS Bedrock and Predibase now offer managed GRPO, significantly narrowing this gap. Bedrock supports Lambda-based reward functions with GRPO. Predibase is serverless. The gap has partially closed since initial identification.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Very High. Requires GPU infrastructure, billing system, security, multi-tenancy. Competing with AWS/Predibase on infrastructure is extremely hard. 6+ months to MVP.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM: ~$2B (cloud ML training). SAM: ~$100M (managed RL training). SOM: ~$5-10M possible but requires massive infrastructure investment. Margins thin competing with hyperscalers.

ranked_gaps.title: Multi-Domain / Multi-Task RL Training Orchestrator
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: An orchestration layer for training models across multiple reasoning domains simultaneously. Handles dataset mixing, domain-specific reward routing, task interference mitigation, and progressive domain addition without catastrophic forgetting.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Teams building general-purpose reasoning models. Companies wanting to add domain reasoning to existing models incrementally.
ranked_gaps.why_now: ROLL from Alibaba supports multi-task RL but is ecosystem-locked. Guru dataset spans 6 domains. Research shows domain mixing matters but tooling is manual. Market is narrow.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: High. Task balancing and interference mitigation are active research problems. Reward routing across domains ~4 weeks. Full orchestrator ~10 weeks.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM: ~$80M (multi-task ML training). SAM: ~$8M (multi-domain RL). SOM: ~$1-2M (niche market).

ranked_gaps.title: Framework Interoperability / Model Portability Layer
ranked_gaps.what_to_build: A conversion layer and standard format for RL training recipes, reward functions, and training state across TRL/OpenRLHF/veRL/NeMo-RL. Enables framework switching mid-training without losing optimizer state or reward function compatibility.
ranked_gaps.who_needs_it: Teams evaluating or switching between frameworks. Organizations running multiple frameworks in parallel.
ranked_gaps.why_now: Framework fragmentation is real but the market is consolidating around TRL + OpenRLHF. HuggingFace model format provides base portability. The pain is real but the market may self-solve as winners emerge.
ranked_gaps.build_complexity: Medium. Reward function adapter layer ~3 weeks. Training state conversion ~4 weeks. Maintaining compatibility as frameworks evolve is the ongoing cost.
ranked_gaps.market_size_estimate: TAM: ~$40M (ML tooling interop). SAM: ~$5M (RL-specific). SOM: ~$0.5-1M (small market, likely becomes a feature of a larger tool).
